canonical_title: One-off request to reformat/return a video transcript verbatim
description: User states they will paste video transcript sections by timestamp and wants them returned 'as it is'. The assistant then returns a long transcript. The transcript's subject matter is a third party building an AI news digest app, but that content is the material being formatted, not a project the user is conducting.
why_it_matters: Demonstrates the false-project failure mode: rich project-like content inside a transcript belongs to a third party and to a pure formatting task, not to the user.
confidence_rationale: User's own words explicitly frame this as a verbatim formatting task; the project content is clearly inside the pasted/returned transcript.
uncertainties: Whether the user is the speaker in the transcript or merely formatting someone else's video
inclusion_rationale: Kept as non-knowledge to record that this conversation contains no user project despite project-shaped transcript content.

canonical_title: Transcript content describing an AI news-digest app build (third-party source material)
description: The returned transcript narrates building a YouTube/AI news digest pipeline (digest agent, curator/ranking agent, email agent, Gmail SMTP). This is source material being reformatted, not the user's stated work with Claude.
why_it_matters: Must be explicitly classified as transcript source material so it does not get mistaken for a user project.
confidence_rationale: The text is delivered as a transcript inside a formatting task; no user ownership statements attach to it.
uncertainties: Identity of the transcript author is unresolved
inclusion_rationale: Recorded as attachment/transcript source role per attachment-classification rules.

canonical_title: One-off advice: how to phrase/defend a networking question about Accenture
description: User asks (three close variants) whether 'what is your definition of Accenture' is a good question to ask someone, wanting to surface their lived experience, and how to defend/improve it.
why_it_matters: A self-contained advice request; not a project despite an inferred job context.
confidence_rationale: Short, single-topic advice exchange with no continuing actions or deliverable.
uncertainties: Exact context (networking event vs internship) not stated by the user
inclusion_rationale: Non-knowledge one-off; recorded so it is not mistaken for a project.

canonical_title: One-off factual question: is overtime pay mandatory in DC (incl. hosts/servers)
description: User asks whether overtime is mandatory in Washington DC, then narrows to hosts/servers (tipped workers). A factual legal-information lookup.
why_it_matters: Pure information request; not a project and not durable user knowledge.
confidence_rationale: Two short factual questions with generic answers; no ownership or deliverable.
uncertainties: Whether the user or someone they know works as a host/server (not stated)
inclusion_rationale: Non-knowledge generic question; recorded for completeness.
